source_jobs.title: Senior Manager, Corporate Development & Investor Relations
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/eadb4172-e32d-48aa-b2d2-1be048f84b9c/1c9ccfe8-7946-4ad5-a384-897fea8db295/Bureau%20Careers%20gamechanger.png]


ABOUT BUREAU

Bureau is a unified risk decisioning platform for Compliance, Fraud, and Transaction risks. Our platform is a single decision-making engine, powered by a 1 billion+ identity knowledge graph. Over 150 Banks, fintechs, retailers, and digital platforms use Bureau to verify identities faster and stop fraud earlier globally.

Bureau has raised $50M+ from renowned Silicon Valley and global investors including Sorenson Capital and PayPal Ventures and is expanding rapidly from APAC to Americas, Europe, and beyond.


WHY BUREAU?

Bureau is building the infrastructure that makes digital identities and transactions safe and trustworthy for billions of people. The mission is big, the problems are complex, and the impact is real.

We hire people who want that level of responsibility. People who move fast, build systems from scratch, and care deeply about turning strategy into execution. If you want predictability or narrow scope, this won't be your place. If you want to shape how a scaling global company operates—keep reading.

What you'll do

To act as the bridge between Bureau’s internal data and the global investment community. You will own the “Financial Story”, manage investor relations, and execute inorganic growth strategies to maintain Bureau’s position as a Unified Risk-Decisioning Platform.

What you'll bring

Essential Qualities:

 1. Experience: 6+ years of total experience with 3 years in an Investment Bank/ Corp Dev/ IR role in a company.

 2. Financial Proficiency: Advanced financial modelling skills for forecasting, fundraising, and Annual Operating Plans.

 3. Strategic Storytelling: Ability to leverage numbers, convert technical details to weave compelling investor narratives.

 4. Strategic vision: Ability to develop a framework for evaluating inorganic opportunities, scout for inorganic opportunities that augment Bureau and the commercial acumen to analyse such opportunities.

 5. Cross-functional coordination: Proven experience in handling projects like fundraising, M&A deals, and holding Investor Days, interacting with cross-functional teams.

Desirable Qualities:

 1. Fintech Experience: An understanding of the fintech ecosystem in India, along with the regulatory shifts.

 2. Startup Experience: An experience of working at an early/ growth stage startup.


OUR CULTURE

 - We hire self-motivated people and get out of their way

 - We value performance, not hours worked

 - Speed, ownership, and impact matter most


COMPENSATION

 - Competitive salary + potential equity

 - Health benefits, flexible PTO, learning budget
   
   

[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/eadb4172-e32d-48aa-b2d2-1be048f84b9c/6fccb083-d8dc-45b1-b1fa-bf1309a7b74b/Bureau%20Careers%20Bottom.png]
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/eadb4172-e32d-48aa-b2d2-1be048f84b9c/94e1a182-4d95-4398-9076-0c819236e8c9/Bureau%20career%20benefits.png]

source_jobs.title: Information Security Engineer
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/eadb4172-e32d-48aa-b2d2-1be048f84b9c/1c9ccfe8-7946-4ad5-a384-897fea8db295/Bureau%20Careers%20gamechanger.png]


ABOUT BUREAU

Bureau is a unified risk decisioning platform for Compliance, Fraud, and Transaction risks. Our platform is a single decision-making engine, powered by a 1 billion+ identity knowledge graph. Over 150 Banks, fintechs, retailers, and digital platforms use Bureau to verify identities faster and stop fraud earlier globally.

Bureau has raised $50M+ from renowned Silicon Valley and global investors including Sorenson Capital and PayPal Ventures and is expanding rapidly from APAC to Americas, Europe, and beyond.


WHY BUREAU?

Bureau is building the infrastructure that makes digital identities and transactions safe and trustworthy for billions of people. The mission is big, the problems are complex, and the impact is real.

We hire people who want that level of responsibility. People who move fast, build systems from scratch, and care deeply about turning strategy into execution. If you want predictability or narrow scope, this won't be your place. If you want to shape how a scaling global company operates—keep reading.


ABOUT THE ROLE - INFORMATION SECURITY ENGINEER

We are looking for a Security Engineer who can own both the hands-on technical security stack and our governance/compliance programs.

What you’ll be doing

In this role, you will:

 - Harden and monitor our cloud & container infrastructure (AWS/EKS, endpoints, network).

 - Run vulnerability management, security tooling and incident response.

 - Help maintain our ISMS and support audits (ISO 27001, SOC 2, RBI, DPDP, etc.).

This is ideal for someone who doesn’t want to be only “checklist GRC” or only “pure blue-team”, but wants a blended role across security engineering + GRC.Key Responsibilities
1. Cloud & Infrastructure Security (Hands-on)

 - Work with DevOps to secure our AWS/EKS environment:
   
   - IAM hardening, security groups, VPC, KMS, S3, RDS, etc.
   
   - Review infra-as-code (Terraform/Helm) for security issues and misconfigurations.

 - Own or co-own key security tools:
   
   - Endpoint / EDR (e.g., CrowdStrike / SentinelOne),
   
   - Cloud security (CSPM / CNAPP, GuardDuty, Security Hub, WAF, etc.),
   
   - Container / runtime security where applicable.

 - Implement and maintain logging & monitoring for security events (CloudTrail, ALB/NLB logs, K8s logs, etc.), and integrate them with SIEM / alerting.

2. Vulnerability Management & Security Operations

 - Own the vulnerability management lifecycle:
   
   - Run periodic scans for cloud, endpoints, containers and apps.
   
   - Triage findings, prioritise based on risk, and drive closure with engineering.

 - Coordinate external pentests / bug bounties and track remediation.

 - Support incident response:
   
   - Help investigate alerts, gather evidence, and contribute to RCA and CAPA.
   
   - Maintain and update incident runbooks.

3. Governance, Risk & Compliance (ISMS, Audits, DPDP)

 - Maintain and enhance the Information Security Management System (ISMS):
   
   - Policies, procedures, SoA, risk register, control evidence and audit trails.

 - Support internal and external audits: ISO 27001, SOC 2, RBI/CERT-In, Data Protection.

 - Prepare and manage audit evidence, observations, closure reports and certification documentation.

 - Assist with risk assessments:
   
   - Maintain the risk register, risk treatment plans and residual risk reviews.
   
   - Conduct vendor security due diligence and maintain vendor security records (MSA, NDA, DPA, DPIA, etc.).

 - Support privacy & regulatory compliance operations (GDPR/DPDP basics: retention, consent, grievance logging).

4. Access, Asset & Control Assurance

 - Participate in and help automate access reviews, asset inventory checks, and configuration compliance checks.

 - Track control performance (vuln SLAs, access reviews, backup tests, etc.) and ensure gaps are docu

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/eadb4172-e32d-48aa-b2d2-1be048f84b9c/1c9ccfe8-7946-4ad5-a384-897fea8db295/Bureau%20Careers%20gamechanger.png]


ABOUT BUREAU

Bureau is a unified risk decisioning platform for Compliance, Fraud, and Transaction risks. Our platform is a single decision-making engine, powered by a 1 billion+ identity knowledge graph. Over 150 Banks, fintechs, retailers, and digital platforms use Bureau to verify identities faster and stop fraud earlier globally.

We hire people who want that level of responsibility. People who move fast, build systems from scratch, and care deeply about turning strategy into execution. If you want predictability or narrow scope, this won't be your place. If you want to shape how a scaling global company operates—keep reading.

About the Role - Senior Product Marketing Manager

Bureau is looking for a Senior Product Marketer who can independently translate our market insight and thought leadership into compelling, tangible go-to-market assets.

You’ll work directly with Sales, Product, and Leadership to define how Bureau tells its story across the Identity, Fraud, and Financial Services ecosystem.

This is a hands-on role that blends strategic clarity with strong execution - ideal for someone who enjoys owning outcomes end-to-end without needing constant direction.

What you’ll be doing

Go-to-Market & Messaging

 - Develop and maintain crisp, differentiated messaging and positioning for Bureau’s solutions across verticals and geographies (India, SEA, MEA).

 - Translate complex product capabilities in identity, fraud, and risk intelligence into simple, customer-focused narratives.

 - Support new product and feature launches with GTM collateral, internal enablement, and post-launch analysis.

Sales & Leadership Enablement

 - Work with the Sales and Leadership teams to convert Bureau’s GTM and thought leadership into decks, case studies, one-pagers, and videos that advance conversations and shorten deal cycles.

 - Build and maintain an asset library aligned to buyer stages and use cases.

 - Partner with Sales to ensure consistent messaging and positioning in every external touchpoint.

Market Intelligence & Insights

 - Research and monitor market trends, competitors, and regulatory developments in financial services, fraud prevention, and digital identity.

 - Feed actionable insights back into Product and GTM strategy to influence roadmap and positioning decisions.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Work closely with Product, Growth, and SDR teams to ensure alignment between market needs and product narratives.

 - Own reporting and metrics on marketing asset usage, win-rates, and adoption by sales teams.

What You’ll Bring

To shine here, you should have:

 - 6 to 10 years of experience in Product Marketing or GTM for Enterprise SaaS, preferably in Identity, Fraud, or BFSI.

 - Proven ability to work independently with Sales and Leadership to conceptualize and produce GTM deliverables without daily supervision.

 - Demonstrated experience in messaging, enablement, and launch execution for B2B tech products.

 - Strong communication and storytelling skills with an analytical mindset.

 - Comfortable working in a high-velocity, entrepreneurial environment with tight cross-functional collaboration.

 - Familiarity with CRM and automation tools such as HubSpot, Salesforce, or equivalent.

Nice to Have

 - Exposure to SEA or Middle East fintech markets.

 - Experience supporting enterprise sales teams with GTM collateral and competitive insights.

 - Previous backgr

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Specialist
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/eadb4172-e32d-48aa-b2d2-1be048f84b9c/1c9ccfe8-7946-4ad5-a384-897fea8db295/Bureau%20Careers%20gamechanger.png]


ABOUT BUREAU

Bureau is a unified risk decisioning platform for Compliance, Fraud, and Transaction risks. Our platform is a single decision-making engine, powered by a 1 billion+ identity knowledge graph. Over 150 Banks, fintechs, retailers, and digital platforms use Bureau to verify identities faster and stop fraud earlier globally.

Bureau has raised $50M+ from renowned Silicon Valley and global investors including Sorenson Capital and PayPal Ventures and is expanding rapidly from APAC to Americas, Europe, and beyond.

Bureau is building the infrastructure that makes digital identities and transactions safe and trustworthy for billions of people. The mission is big, the problems are complex, and the impact is real.

We hire people who want that level of responsibility. People who move fast, build systems from scratch, and care deeply about turning strategy into execution. If you want predictability or narrow scope, this won't be your place. If you want to shape how a scaling global company operates—keep reading.

About the Role - Product Marketing Specialist

We’re looking for an Product Marketing Specialist who is eager to learn by doing. You’ll be crucial in translating Bureau’s product capabilities and market insight into compelling GTM assets - collateral, messaging, and enablement tools that help Sales close faster and help the market understand what Bureau does and why it matters.

This is a hands-on execution role. You’ll be deep in the details - drafting decks, researching competitors, supporting product launches, and working closely with Sales, Product, and Growth.

What you’ll be doing

GTM Support & Messaging

 - Support the development of messaging and positioning for Bureau’s solutions - learning to translate complex product capabilities into clear, customer-first narratives.

 - Help execute new product and feature launches with GTM collateral, internal briefing notes, and post-launch tracking.

 - Draft and iterate on one-pagers, battlecards, case studies, decks, and email and website copy - producing assets that the Sales/Growth team can pick up and use immediately.

Sales Enablement

 - Work with the Marketing, Sales, and Product teams to build and maintain an asset library aligned to buyer stages, verticals, and use cases.

 - Ensure messaging consistency across external touchpoints - from pitch decks to nurture sequences.

 - Support the preparation of leadership and board-level presentations where product narrative is involved.

Market Intelligence

 - Research and monitor competitors, market trends, and regulatory developments in identity, fraud prevention, and financial services.

 - Maintain up-to-date competitive intelligence that the Sales team can reference in live deals.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 3-5 years of experience in product marketing, or GTM for a B2B SaaS or Enterprise tech company.

 - Proficient in using AI tools (Claude, Gemini, or equivalent) to accelerate content creation, research synthesis - you should know when to use AI as a force multiplier and when human judgment takes over.

 - Thrives in a high-velocity environment where priorities shift and speed matters.

 - Prior experience in identity, fraud, fintech, or BFSI, or a strong appetite to get up to speed fast.

 - Background supporting enterprise sales teams with competitive or GTM collateral.

 - Exposure to SEA or Middle East markets.


OUR CULTURE

 - We hire self-motivated people and get out of their way

 - We value performance, not hours worked

 - Speed, ownership, and impact matter most


COMPENSATION

 - Competitive salary + potential equity

 - Health benefits, flexible PTO, learning budget

source_jobs.title: Product Manager (Transaction Monitoring)
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/eadb4172-e32d-48aa-b2d2-1be048f84b9c/1c9ccfe8-7946-4ad5-a384-897fea8db295/Bureau%20Careers%20gamechanger.png]


ABOUT BUREAU

Bureau is a unified risk decisioning platform for Compliance, Fraud, and Transaction risks. Our platform is a single decision-making engine, powered by a 1 billion+ identity knowledge graph. Over 150 Banks, fintechs, retailers, and digital platforms use Bureau to verify identities faster and stop fraud earlier globally.

Bureau has raised $50M+ from renowned Silicon Valley and global investors including Sorenson Capital and PayPal Ventures and is expanding rapidly from APAC to Americas, Europe, and beyond.


WHY BUREAU?

Bureau is building the infrastructure that makes digital identities and transactions safe and trustworthy for billions of people. The mission is big, the problems are complex, and the impact is real.

We hire people who want that level of responsibility. People who move fast, build systems from scratch, and care deeply about turning strategy into execution. If you want predictability or narrow scope, this won't be your place. If you want to shape how a scaling global company operates—keep reading.


POSITION OVERVIEW

We're looking for an experienced and proactive Product Manager - Transaction Monitoring to join our dynamic team. This role requires a unique blend of "hacker" energy, deep compliance knowledge, and extreme customer empathy. The role combines deep domain expertise with strong client-facing responsibilities, focusing on helping organizations combat evolving fraud threats through advanced risk management solutions and the strategic application of our product. If you thrive on tackling complex challenges, enjoy translating technical solutions into tangible business value, and excel in a collaborative environment, we want to hear from you.


WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

As a Product Manager, you'll:

 - Vision & Strategy: Define the roadmap for our first-generation TM system, moving from manual spreadsheets to automated, AI-native, rule-based, and ML-driven detection.

 - Technical Architecture: Partner with Engineering to design a scalable real-time risk engine capable of handling high transaction volumes with low latency.

 - Feature Ownership: Lead the ideation and launch of core modules: Rule Engines, Alert Management, Case Investigation Workflows, and SAR (Suspicious Activity Report) filing.

 - Customer Empathy: Act as the "voice of the customer." Spend time with our internal Compliance and Ops teams to understand their pain points and build tools that make them 10x more efficient.

 - Serve as the primary functional and product point of contact for customer requirements, overseeing successful solution implementations and product adoption.

 - Friction Reduction: Design "smart" monitoring that reduces false positives, ensuring that good customers aren't caught in the net of over-sensitive triggers.

 - Feedback Loops: Establish a continuous loop where customer feedback directly influences the product iteration cycle.

 - Regulatory Alignment: Stay ahead of AML/BSA, KYC, and Sanctions regulations to ensure the product is "compliant by design."

 - Typology Development: Identify and build detection logic for emerging financial crime patterns (e.g., structuring, rapid movement of funds, mule accounts).

 - Vendor Management: Evaluate and integrate third-party data providers for watchlist screening and identity verification.

 - Consult with potential and existing clients to develop and implement customized fraud prevention rules and strategies using our product suite.

 - Analyze emerging fraud patterns and adapt detection methodologies, advising clients on how our product capabilities can address these evolving threats.

 - Conduct impactful pre-sales demonstrations and provide expert product consultations for prospective clients, clearly articulating our product's value proposition.

 - Troubleshoot and debug existing transactiona

source_jobs.title: Group Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/eadb4172-e32d-48aa-b2d2-1be048f84b9c/1c9ccfe8-7946-4ad5-a384-897fea8db295/Bureau%20Careers%20gamechanger.png]


ABOUT BUREAU

Bureau is a unified risk decisioning platform for Compliance, Fraud, and Transaction risks. Our platform is a single decision-making engine, powered by a 1 billion+ identity knowledge graph. Over 150 Banks, fintechs, retailers, and digital platforms use Bureau to verify identities faster and stop fraud earlier globally.

Bureau has raised $50M+ from renowned Silicon Valley and global investors including Sorenson Capital and PayPal Ventures and is expanding rapidly from APAC to Americas, Europe, and beyond.


WHY BUREAU?

Bureau is building the infrastructure that makes digital identities and transactions safe and trustworthy for billions of people. The mission is big, the problems are complex, and the impact is real.

We hire people who want that level of responsibility. People who move fast, build systems from scratch, and care deeply about turning strategy into execution. If you want predictability or narrow scope, this won't be your place. If you want to shape how a scaling global company operates—keep reading.

About the Role - Group Product Manager

We are seeking a seasoned and strategic Group Product Manager to own the vision, strategy, and execution for our core Fraud & Risk product line, with a specific focus on Device Intelligence and Behavioral Biometrics. This is a pivotal, high-impact role responsible for defining the future of products that are fundamental to our customers' security and our company's growth.

You will be the catalyst for transforming our advanced fraud detection technologies into a globally scalable, market-leading solution. The ideal candidate will have deep domain expertise in the fraud and identity space, a proven track record of shipping successful B2B SaaS products, and a relentless passion for solving complex customer problems

What you’ll be doing

 - Own the Product Vision & Strategy: Define and evangelize a clear, compelling vision and multi-year strategy for the Device Intelligence and Behavioral Biometrics product lines, aligning with Bureau's mission and international growth objectives.

 - Drive the Product Roadmap: Develop and manage a prioritized roadmap that balances innovation, competitive differentiation, customer needs, and strategic business goals. You will be responsible for making tough prioritization decisions and communicating the "why" behind them.

 - Lead Product Discovery & Delivery: Collaborate intensively with Engineering, Data Science, and Design partners to translate the roadmap into clear requirements. Lead the team through the full product lifecycle, from discovery and validation to launch and iteration.

 - Be the Voice of the Customer: Engage directly with customers, prospects, and internal stakeholders to deeply understand market needs, emerging fraud patterns, and competitive dynamics. Translate these insights into actionable product initiatives.

 - Enable Go-To-Market Success: Partner closely with Sales, Marketing, and Revenue Operations to develop compelling positioning, define pricing & packaging, and create the necessary collateral to ensure the commercial success of your products in both our core Indian market and new international regions.

 - Define and Measure Success: Establish and analyze key product metrics to measure performance, drive adoption, and inform future product decisions.

What You’ll Bring

 - Minimum of ideally 6-10 years of hands-on product management experience in a B2B SaaS environment. What matters more than years of experience is the ability to operate at a Group Product Manager level, driving strategy, influencing stakeholders, and delivering outcomes through strong product leadership.

 - Experience building and owning technical B2B products such as APIs, SDKs, data platforms, risk engines, decisioning systems, or developer-facing SaaS products.

 - Strong

source_jobs.title: Integration Engineer (SDK)
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/eadb4172-e32d-48aa-b2d2-1be048f84b9c/1c9ccfe8-7946-4ad5-a384-897fea8db295/Bureau%20Careers%20gamechanger.png]


ABOUT BUREAU

Bureau is a unified risk decisioning platform for Compliance, Fraud, and Transaction risks. Our platform is a single decision-making engine, powered by a 1 billion+ identity knowledge graph. Over 150 Banks, fintechs, retailers, and digital platforms use Bureau to verify identities faster and stop fraud earlier globally.

Bureau has raised $50M+ from renowned Silicon Valley and global investors including Sorenson Capital and PayPal Ventures and is expanding rapidly from APAC to Americas, Europe, and beyond.


WHY BUREAU?

Bureau is building the infrastructure that makes digital identities and transactions safe and trustworthy for billions of people. The mission is big, the problems are complex, and the impact is real.

We hire people who want that level of responsibility. People who move fast, build systems from scratch, and care deeply about turning strategy into execution. If you want predictability or narrow scope, this won't be your place. If you want to shape how a scaling global company operates—keep reading.

About the Role - Integration Engineer

Are you passionate about solving complex technical challenges? Join our team as an SDK Integration Engineer and be at the forefront of our clients' success. In this role, you will serve as the technical bridge between our SDK products and our clients, guiding external development teams through seamless Web and Mobile SDK integrations. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams—including Core Engineering, Product, Sales, and Solutions—to ensure a frictionless customer experience.

What you’ll be doing

 - Integration Support: Lead the resolution of SDK integration queries, acting as the primary technical contact and subject matter expert for customers.

 - Customer Experience: Elevate Customer satisfaction through proactive onboarding support, architectural guidance, and quick resolution of integration blockers.

 - Code-Level Troubleshooting: Diagnose complex implementation issues by debugging client code, analyzing network requests, and reviewing SDK behavior across Web and Mobile platforms.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with SDK Engineering and Product teams to channel feedback, report bugs, and advocate for SDK usability and performance improvements.

 - Technical Consulting: Partner with pre-sales and onboarding teams to provide technical demonstrations, feasibility assessments, and best practices for integrating our SDKs into diverse technology stacks.

 - Documentation & Resources: Create, maintain, and enhance comprehensive SDK documentation, integration guides, code snippets, and fully functional sample applications (via GitHub) to streamline client onboarding.

 - Integration Tooling & Automation: Build and maintain internal tools, scripts, and automated workflows to validate client implementations, scale support operations, and reduce time-to-resolution.

 - Proactive Analytics & Optimization: Utilize advanced analytics and monitoring tools to proactively detect emerging platform issues, identify systemic integration trends, and optimize our self-service developer portals.

 - Project Management: Contribute to strategic technical projects that enhance the overall customer journey and add value to the organization's API/SDK ecosystem.

What You’ll Bring

 - Experience: 2–6 years in SDK Integration, Developer Support, Solutions Engineering, or Mobile/Web Development, preferably in a B2B SaaS.

 - Web & Mobile Proficiency: Deep understanding of Web technologies (JavaScript, TypeScript, HTML/CSS) and Mobile ecosystems (native iOS/Swift/Objective-C, native Android/Kotlin/Java, or cross-platform frameworks like React Native/Flutter).

 - Advanced Debugging Skills: Expertise in using developer tools (Chrome DevTools, Xcode, Android Studio), network proxies, and API testing platf

source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/eadb4172-e32d-48aa-b2d2-1be048f84b9c/1c9ccfe8-7946-4ad5-a384-897fea8db295/Bureau%20Careers%20gamechanger.png]


ABOUT BUREAU

Bureau is a unified risk decisioning platform for Compliance, Fraud, and Transaction risks. Our platform is a single decision-making engine, powered by a 1 billion+ identity knowledge graph. Over 150 Banks, fintechs, retailers, and digital platforms use Bureau to verify identities faster and stop fraud earlier globally.

Bureau has raised $50M+ from renowned Silicon Valley and global investors including Sorenson Capital and PayPal Ventures and is expanding rapidly from APAC to Americas, Europe, and beyond.


WHY BUREAU?

Bureau is building the infrastructure that makes digital identities and transactions safe and trustworthy for billions of people. The mission is big, the problems are complex, and the impact is real.

We hire people who want that level of responsibility. People who move fast, build systems from scratch, and care deeply about turning strategy into execution. If you want predictability or narrow scope, this won't be your place. If you want to shape how a scaling global company operates—keep reading.

As a Product Designer at Bureau, you will shape how users visualize, manage, and optimize risk decisions. You’ll be responsible for the end-to-end UX/UI for sophisticated capabilities, including workflow builder, orchestration, behavioural biometrics UI, rule configuration, and Device ID reporting. Your designs will bring clarity, trust, and precision to decision-making in high-stakes environments.

What you’ll be doing

 - Translate complex orchestration flows, rule engines, and case management dashboards into intuitive, user-friendly designs that empower risk, product, and engineering teams

 - Create end user experience to build advanced, super clean and intuitive onboarding and verification journey for large enterprises

 - Create seamless experiences for fraud, risk, and security management platforms.

 - Build and maintain design systems, components, and style guidelines to ensure consistency across Bureau’s multi-signal platform.

 - Work closely with Product, Engineering, and Data teams to translate AI-powered workflows into cohesive visual experiences; ensure feasibility and fidelity throughout the design journey.

 - Conduct research and usability studies with real users in risk, product, and compliance functions to iterate interfaces that deliver clarity, speed, and trust.

 - Provide pixel-perfect assets and design specs that accelerate development and elevate execution quality.

What You’ll bring

 - 2-3 years of hands-on experience as a Product Designer in enterprise SaaS, ecommerce, or FinTech.

 - Experience in building customer facing user journeys for B2C brands.

 - Expertise in crafting visually compelling, UI-rich mockups with a strong sense of design taste.

 - Ability to translate Bureau’s brand values in all the clear design solutions.

 - Demonstrated ability to design complex data-rich interfaces, such as dashboards, workflow builders, or admin tooling.

 - Mastery of Figma/Sketch/Adobe XD, prototyping tools (like Framer, rive and/or lottie), and collaborative design systems.

 - Strong UI/UX sense with emphasis on information architecture, interaction design, data visualization, and accessibility.

 - Resolute attention to detail, advocacy for usability and accessibility, and passion for turning complex problems into elegant solutions.

Bonus Points

 - AI Efficiency: Demonstrated ability to use AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Midjourney, or Figma AI plugins) to accelerate research, UX writing, or workflow automation.

 - Motion Design: Experience creating micro-interactions to enhance the user experience.

 - Technical Foundation: Basic knowledge of HTML/CSS or how front-end frameworks operate.

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/eadb4172-e32d-48aa-b2d2-1be048f84b9c/1c9ccfe8-7946-4ad5-a384-897fea8db295/Bureau%20Careers%20gamechanger.png]


ABOUT BUREAU

Bureau is a unified risk decisioning platform for Compliance, Fraud, and Transaction risks. Our platform is a single decision-making engine, powered by a 1 billion+ identity knowledge graph. Over 150 Banks, fintechs, retailers, and digital platforms use Bureau to verify identities faster and stop fraud earlier globally.

Bureau has raised $50M+ from renowned Silicon Valley and global investors including Sorenson Capital and PayPal Ventures and is expanding rapidly from APAC to Americas, Europe, and beyond.


WHY BUREAU?

Bureau is building the infrastructure that makes digital identities and transactions safe and trustworthy for billions of people. The mission is big, the problems are complex, and the impact is real.

We hire people who want that level of responsibility. People who move fast, build systems from scratch, and care deeply about turning strategy into execution. If you want predictability or narrow scope, this won't be your place. If you want to shape how a scaling global company operates—keep reading.


ABOUT THE ROLE - BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT REPRESENTATIVE

Bureau is looking for a busines Development representative (BDR) professional who sets ambitious personal goals with a consistent and repeatable process. You will be instrumental in landing and expanding our core customer base. You will be working with senior-level technical and non-technical stakeholders across multiple verticals. In addition to managing the full sales cycle, you will have a significant opportunity to shape our GTM processes.

What you’ll be doing

 - You will be responsible for sourcing new sales opportunities through inbound lead follow-up and outbound campaigns through email, phone & social platforms to build a great first expression.

 - You will have to understand customer needs and requirements and qualify leads.

 - You will need to research accounts, identify key stakeholders & generate interest.

 - You will be maintaining and expanding your database of prospects.

 - You will team with field & channel sales teams to build pipelines and close deals.

 - You will be conducting effective online demos for prospects.

 - You will route qualified opportunities to the appropriate sales managers for further development, nurturing and closure.

 - Research potential target markets based on our product and technology offerings and on the overall markets' needs, and create actionable target prospects lists, also leveraging an existing network

 - Work efficiently both independently and as a member of a cross-functional team.

 - You will be at the forefront of working with the market development team through a period of hypergrowth.

What You’ll Bring

 - 3-4 years of work experience in Business Development or Sales for API-based, fast paced/hyper growth SaaS companies with hands-on experience in Lead generation.

 - Familiarity across the entire customer acquisition cycle.

 - Experience solving problems with minimal guidance and supervision, and you proactively identify new ways to improve productivity and efficiency.

 - Passion for being with customers, telling their stories, expanding relationships, and doing so in a strategic manner.

 - Experience supporting BFSI, Tech, Fintech, Gaming, etc. industry segments.

 - Ability to solve problems with minimal guidance and supervision and you proactively identify new ways to improve productivity and efficiency

 - Continually pursue professional and personal growth, you are able to receive and act on feedback - as well as give constructive feedback.

 - Exceptional communication skills, ability to express ideas and information clearly and explain ideas logically.

 - Strong collaboration and interpersonal skills, easily build rapport and establish relationships with both customers and colleagues

 - Ability to learn a techni

source_jobs.title: Director, Revenue Operations
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/eadb4172-e32d-48aa-b2d2-1be048f84b9c/1c9ccfe8-7946-4ad5-a384-897fea8db295/Bureau%20Careers%20gamechanger.png]


ABOUT BUREAU

Bureau is a unified risk decisioning platform for Compliance, Fraud, and Transaction risks. Our platform is a single decision-making engine, powered by a 1 billion+ identity knowledge graph. Over 150 Banks, fintechs, retailers, and digital platforms use Bureau to verify identities faster and stop fraud earlier globally.

Bureau has raised $50M+ from renowned Silicon Valley and global investors including Sorenson Capital and PayPal Ventures and is expanding rapidly from APAC to Americas, Europe, and beyond.


WHY BUREAU?

Bureau is building the infrastructure that makes digital identities and transactions safe and trustworthy for billions of people. The mission is big, the problems are complex, and the impact is real.

We hire people who want that level of responsibility. People who move fast, build systems from scratch, and care deeply about turning strategy into execution. If you want predictability or narrow scope, this won't be your place. If you want to shape how a scaling global company operates—keep reading.

What you'll do

To architect and manage the end-to-end revenue lifecycle, ensuring that every dollar of GTM spend results in predictable, high-margin ARR and minimises the gap between marketing leads and recognised revenue.

What you'll bring

Essential Qualities:

 1. Experience: 8 years of total experience with 4+ years in a RevOps role at a B2B SaaS company.

 2. Analytical rigour: Ability to analyse key metrics such as LTV: CAC, and Magic Numbers

 3. Systems Architecture: Proficiency in CRM tools (Hubspot/ Salesforce) and ability to build a single source of truth.

 4. Commercial Acumen: Ability to set up and operationalise a deal desk function and experience in structuring complex software deals for enterprises.

 5. Operational Resilience: Proven record in handling revenue recognition and complex reconciliations for smooth translation to financial statements with a strong attention to detail.

 6. Strategic Communication: Ability to analyse patterns and devise solutions for GTM, sales strategy, to prepare reviews and drive executive alignment and decision-making.

Desirable Qualities:

 1. Fintech/ Fraud Expertise: A keen understanding of the identity, risk, payments and orchestration space.

 2. International Exposure: Experience in managing RevOps internationally, beyond India.

 3. Enterprise sales cycle: Experience in handling pricing/ RevOps targeting enterprises, including participation in RFPs.


OUR CULTURE

 - We hire self-motivated people and get out of their way

 - We value performance, not hours worked

 - Speed, ownership, and impact matter most


COMPENSATION

 - Competitive salary + potential equity

 - Health benefits, flexible PTO, learning budget

source_jobs.title: Legal Associate
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/eadb4172-e32d-48aa-b2d2-1be048f84b9c/1c9ccfe8-7946-4ad5-a384-897fea8db295/Bureau%20Careers%20gamechanger.png]


ABOUT BUREAU

Bureau is a unified risk decisioning platform for Compliance, Fraud, and Transaction risks. Our platform is a single decision-making engine, powered by a 1 billion+ identity knowledge graph. Over 150 Banks, fintechs, retailers, and digital platforms use Bureau to verify identities faster and stop fraud earlier globally.

Bureau has raised $50M+ from renowned Silicon Valley and global investors including Sorenson Capital and PayPal Ventures and is expanding rapidly from APAC to Americas, Europe, and beyond.


WHY BUREAU?

Bureau is building the infrastructure that makes digital identities and transactions safe and trustworthy for billions of people. The mission is big, the problems are complex, and the impact is real.

We hire people who want that level of responsibility. People who move fast, build systems from scratch, and care deeply about turning strategy into execution. If you want predictability or narrow scope, this won't be your place. If you want to shape how a scaling global company operates—keep reading.

What you’ll be doing

 - Draft, review, negotiate, and redline a range of commercial contracts, including SaaS, data protection, reseller, partnership, vendor, and employment-related agreements.

 - Provide clear, business-oriented legal advice to internal stakeholders on day-to-day operational matters, contract structuring, and risk mitigation.

 - Advise on legal matters relating to employment law, intellectual property, and product-related issues.

 - Liaise with external legal counsel and manage internal workflows to ensure timely closure of legal matters.

 - Maintain contract and legal trackers, MIS, and documentation systems to ensure up-to-date records and streamlined reporting.

 - Conduct legal research and analysis on evolving laws and regulations impacting the company.

 - Participate in broader legal projects and cross-functional initiatives as required.

 - Uphold a solution-oriented mindset and proactively identify process improvements and operational efficiencies.

What You’ll Bring

 - LL.B. from a recognised law school in India with 1–2 years of post-qualification experience in a start-up, reg-tech, fintech, bank, or MNC environment, with a strong commercial and employment law background.

 - Strong experience in contract drafting, redlining, negotiation, and legal risk assessment.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, with the ability to simplify legal language for business stakeholders.

 - Strong organisational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to independently manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

 - Proficiency in MS Office and comfort using modern productivity and AI-based tools to streamline routine legal tasks.

 - High integrity, accountability, and a solution-oriented mindset.

 - Ability to work cross-functionally and collaboratively while taking full ownership of assigned matters from start to finish.


OUR CULTURE

 - We hire self-motivated people and get out of their way

 - We value performance, not hours worked

 - Speed, ownership, and impact matter most


COMPENSATION

 - Competitive salary + potential equity

 - Health benefits, flexible PTO, learning budget
   
   

[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/eadb4172-e32d-48aa-b2d2-1be048f84b9c/6fccb083-d8dc-45b1-b1fa-bf1309a7b74b/Bureau%20Careers%20Bottom.png]
